title: Add currency selection option in Settings

requirements.summary: Add a currency selector control in Settings screen that persists selection to backend and local storage
acceptanceCriteria: Settings screen shows a clearly labeled 'Currency' section with three selectable options: USD ($), INR (₹), JPY (¥)
The currently selected currency is visually highlighted
Selecting a currency calls the backend setCurrency method and updates local state
The selected currency persists across page refreshes

file_operations.description: Add a Currency section with a radio group or select control offering three options: US Dollar (USD / $), Indian Rupee (INR / ₹), and Japanese Yen (JPY / ¥). Load the current currency preference from the backend on mount using getCurrencyPreference(). When the user selects a currency, call setCurrencyPreference() on the backend actor and also store the value in localStorage for persistence across refreshes. Display the currently selected currency as highlighted/checked.

Create a custom hook that manages currency state globally. Use React Query to fetch the current currency preference via getCurrencyPreference() and provide a mutation for setCurrencyPreference(). Sync the currency value with localStorage so it persists across page refreshes. Export the current currency, loading state, and a setter function for use across the app.

Define a constant array of currency objects with code (USD/INR/JPY), symbol ($, ₹, ¥), and locale (en-US, en-IN, ja-JP) properties. Export a helper function that returns the symbol and locale for a given currency code.

requirements.summary: Update all currency formatting across the app to use the user's selected currency symbol and locale formatting
acceptanceCriteria: All displayed monetary amounts use the symbol matching the selected currency ($ / ₹ / ¥)
Number formatting uses the appropriate locale for the selected currency
Changing currency in Settings immediately reflects across all screens without requiring a page reload

file_operations.description: Update the formatAmount and formatExpenseAmount utility functions to accept a currency parameter (USD, INR, or JPY) and use the corresponding locale and symbol from the currencies constants. Use Intl.NumberFormat with the appropriate locale (en-US for USD, en-IN for INR, ja-JP for JPY) to format amounts. Ensure all call sites of these functions pass the current currency from the useCurrency hook.
Import and use the useCurrency hook to get the current currency. Pass the currency value to the formatAmount utility when displaying total spent and average spend amounts.
Import and use the useCurrency hook to get the current currency. Pass the currency value to the formatAmount utility when displaying category totals.
Import and use the useCurrency hook to get the current currency. Pass the currency value to the formatAmount utility when displaying each expense amount in the list.
Import and use the useCurrency hook to get the current currency. Pass the currency value to the formatAmount utility when displaying the session total and individual entry amounts.
Import and use the useCurrency hook to get the current currency. Pass the currency value to the formatAmount utility when displaying each calculator entry amount.
Import and use the useCurrency hook to get the current currency. If the form displays any formatted amount previews, pass the currency value to the formatting utility.

Import and use the useCurrency hook to get the current currency. If the form displays any formatted amount previews or hints, pass the currency value to the formatting utility. Add a currency symbol prefix or hint in the amount input field to show the active currency.
